page: Microsoft Azure E96ns_v6 is an Env6-family virtual machine configured with 96 dedicated vCPUs on an x86_64 Intel processor architecture. Running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance features 768.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, relying instead on external network-attached storage solutions.

This server is designated as network-optimized and is premium storage capable, allowing high-throughput connections to Azure Premium SSDs. The lack of local storage means all persistent data must be hosted on attached network volumes, which is a key architectural tradeoff to consider for disk-intensive operations.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E96ns_v6 offers a dense memory profile relative to its compute resources, making it suitable for enterprise workloads that require substantial RAM without the overhead of local NVMe drives. Recommended workloads include large-scale relational databases, in-memory caching layers, enterprise resource planning systems, and memory-intensive data processing pipelines.
description: Microsoft Azure E96ns_v6 is an Env6-family virtual machine featuring 96 dedicated vCPUs on an x86_64 Intel processor architecture and 768.0 GB of system memory. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this network-optimized instance provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The server does not include local storage or onboard GPUs, but it is premium storage capable, enabling high-performance connections to external storage volumes. This hardware configuration is designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads, such as large relational databases, in-memory analytics, and data processing pipelines, where high memory density and network throughput are prioritized over local disk storage.
